summary: Orbital floor fracture is the most likely diagnosis because it commonly occurs after blunt trauma with characteristic symptoms including proptosis, pain, and peri-orbital bruising appearing within days—consistent with this patient’s findings. It differs significantly from carotid-cavernous fistula, which involves acute vascular abnormalities not seen clinically herein.
